project: UP Platform Phase 6: Plugin Migration & Ecosystem
description: Port the best claude-code plugins to UP plugin format, create a plugin marketplace registry, and add the skill pipeline orchestration that chains learn -> product-loop automatically.

userStories.title: Port code-review plugin to UP format
userStories.description: Migrate claude-code's code-review plugin into a UP plugin that works with any AI backend. Adds adversarial review as an optional post-verify step in the SESRC loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .up/plugins/installed/code-review/ with plugin.json (engines: ['all'])
Create commands/review.md slash command with YAML frontmatter
Create hooks/post_verify.py that triggers adversarial review after verification passes
Review hook uses AIEngine to prompt a second-opinion review of changed files
Confidence scoring: issues scored 0-100, only issues >= 80 reported
Hook is optional: configurable in .up/config.json: review.enabled (default false)
Output: Rich table of findings with severity, file, line, description
Unit tests with mocked AIEngine review responses
userStories.notes: First real plugin migration. Proves the plugin system works with complex functionality.

userStories.title: Port security-guidance plugin to UP format
userStories.description: Migrate claude-code's security-guidance plugin into a UP plugin that detects security anti-patterns in AI-generated code.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .up/plugins/installed/security-guidance/ with plugin.json
Create rules/ directory with Markdown rule files for each security pattern
Rules cover: eval() injection, os.system() usage, SQL injection patterns, XSS (innerHTML/dangerouslySetInnerHTML), pickle deserialization, hardcoded secrets regex
Each rule is a standalone .md file with pattern regex and warning message
Triggers on POST_TOOL_USE for file write/edit operations
Session-scoped state: don't repeat same warning twice per session
Unit tests with code samples containing each vulnerability pattern
userStories.notes: Good test of the Markdown rules engine from Phase 1.

userStories.title: Port commit-commands plugin to UP format
userStories.description: Migrate claude-code's commit-commands plugin into a UP plugin providing /commit and /commit-push-pr slash commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .up/plugins/installed/git-workflow/ with plugin.json
Create commands/commit.md: stages changes, generates commit message via AI, commits
Create commands/push-pr.md: pushes branch, creates PR via gh CLI
Commands use allowed-tools restrictions for safety (only git and gh commands)
Provenance recorded for each commit (links AI operation to git SHA)
Works with both Claude Code and Cursor (engine-agnostic)
Integration test: mock git operations, verify command flow
userStories.notes: Tests slash command system from Phase 1.

userStories.title: Plugin marketplace registry
userStories.description: Create a marketplace registry that catalogs available plugins (local and remote) with metadata, categories, and version tracking. Foundation for plugin discovery and sharing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/up/plugins/marketplace.py with Marketplace class
MarketplaceEntry dataclass: name, description, version, author, source (local path or git URL), category, downloads (int), rating (float)
marketplace.json schema at .up/plugins/marketplace.json with entries array
Methods: search(query) -> List[MarketplaceEntry], get(name) -> MarketplaceEntry, add(entry), remove(name)
Local registry: scans .up/plugins/installed/ and .up/plugins/builtin/ to build catalog
Remote registry support: reads from a URL (configurable in .up/config.json: marketplace.registry_url)
Unit tests in tests/test_marketplace.py
userStories.notes: Foundation for plugin ecosystem. Remote registry is optional — local-first.

userStories.title: CLI command: up plugin search
userStories.description: Add 'up plugin search' that queries the marketplace registry to find available plugins by keyword or category.
acceptanceCriteria: 'up plugin search <query>' searches by name and description
'up plugin search --category safety' filters by category
Rich table output: Name | Version | Category | Description | Source
Shows installed status: [installed] tag for already-installed plugins
If remote registry configured: searches both local and remote
If no remote: searches local only with message about configuring remote
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004. Simple CLI wrapper around Marketplace.search().

userStories.title: Remote plugin install from git URL
userStories.description: Extend 'up plugin install' to support git URLs, cloning the plugin repo into .up/plugins/installed/.
acceptanceCriteria: 'up plugin install https://github.com/user/my-plugin.git' clones repo
'up plugin install github:user/my-plugin' shorthand syntax supported
Clones to temp dir first, validates plugin.json, then moves to .up/plugins/installed/
Records source URL in registry.json for update tracking
Error handling: invalid URL, missing plugin.json, network failure
Auto-runs 'up sync' after install
userStories.notes: Depends on Phase 1 US-011. Extends local install to support remote sources.

userStories.title: Skill pipeline orchestration: learn -> product-loop
userStories.description: Add automatic handoff between the learning system and product loop. When 'up learn' generates a prd.json, the product loop auto-detects and offers to start execution.
acceptanceCriteria: After 'up learn auto' completes and writes prd.json: prompt user 'PRD ready. Start product loop? [y/n]'
If yes: automatically invoke 'up start' with the generated prd.json
Add --auto-start flag to 'up learn': skips prompt, starts loop immediately
Product loop's OBSERVE phase checks for prd.json freshness (modified within last hour)
If prd.json is stale: warns user and asks to confirm
Emit LEARNING_COMPLETE event when learn finishes, LOOP_START event when product loop begins
Integration test: mock learn output, verify loop starts with correct PRD
userStories.notes: Closes the gap between research and execution. The full pipeline: learn -> prd.json -> product-loop.

userStories.title: Bisect skill as plugin
userStories.description: Package the existing 'up bisect' functionality as a plugin with a slash command, making it available inside AI coding sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .up/plugins/installed/bisect/ with plugin.json
Create commands/bisect.md slash command: walks through UP checkpoints to find which change broke tests
Command accepts: test command to run at each checkpoint (default from .up/config.json)
Uses existing bisect.py logic, wrapped as plugin command
Output: Rich display showing checkpoint walk with pass/fail at each step
Final output: 'Regression introduced in checkpoint {hash}: {commit message}'
Works with both Claude Code and Cursor
userStories.notes: Simple packaging of existing functionality as a plugin.

userStories.title: Plugin template for 'up new'
userStories.description: Add a 'claude-code-plugin' template to 'up new' that scaffolds a complete plugin with example command, hook, rule, and tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/up/templates/projects/plugin/ with template files
'up new my-plugin --template plugin' creates a standalone plugin project
Template includes: plugin.json, commands/example.md, hooks/hooks.json + example hook, rules/example-rule.md, tests/, README.md
README explains plugin structure, how to test, how to install
Example hook is a working Python script with JSON stdin/stdout
Example rule is a working Markdown rule that warns on TODO comments
userStories.notes: Lowers barrier to plugin creation. Template teaches by example.
